rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: NES-era platformer pacing with graduated combat and mobility challenges; tension builds through enemy density and time mechanics rather than continuous escalation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to platforming and basic enemies; establishes movement vocabulary
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of grenades and glow power-ups; enemy density increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Time pressure mechanics emphasized; boss encounters with acquired weapons
notable_spikes: First grenade/glow power-up encounter requiring tactical weapon use

notable_level_patterns.description: Grenades and glow power-ups are unlocked via gameplay progression rather than found in levels, structuring difficulty through equipment availability
Multiple password codes allow players to skip progression (grenades + unlimited ammo/time/lives); suggests intentional difficulty scaling that can be bypassed

sources_used: tcrf: James Bond Jr. (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
extraction_notes: Source material contains development/technical data but minimal level design documentation. Element introduction order inferred from cheat code availability and gameplay progression mechanics. Spatial and rhythm patterns not explicitly documented in available source; marked null where unsupported. Paradigm confirmed as side-scrolling platformer.
